"[[Kingpin|Fisk]] had her killed. He killed Julie, so he could replace her as your North Star.|Benjamin Poindexter and [[Daredevil]]|A New Napkin}} Poindexter received a call from [[Felix Manning]], which turned out to be [[Daredevil]], who mentioned Poindexter killing birds as a child and wondered what [[Eileen Mercer]] would think of him now, but he was unfazed. Daredevil then brought up [[Julie Barnes]] and that she would be disappointed, which infuriated Poindexter and he threatened the vigilante. However, Daredevil revealed that [[Kingpin]] had killed Barnes, which only angered him more. [[File:PoindexterAimingGun-AtTheDark.png|{{AltFloat}}|Poindexter enters in an abandoned building]] Daredevil gave Poindexter an address, so he headed there and entered an abandoned restaurant. Checking the freezer, Poindexter found several frozen bodies, including those of the [[Cleaning Crew]] and Barnes. Horrified by the discovery and realizing that [[Ray Nadeem|Nadeem]] and Daredevil were right about Kingpin, Poindexter screamed in rage. Poindexter then got a call from Daredevil, who he threatened, believing that he was the one who killed her, but Daredevil restated that Kingpin was the one who killed Barnes. [[File:PoindexterBeingCalledByDaredevil-Freezer.png|{{AltFloat}}|Poindexter finds [[Julie Barnes]]' frozen body]] Daredevil explained how Kingpin manipulated Poindexter, such as giving Barnes the job at the [[Presidential Hotel]] and knowing he stalked her, only to have her killed so he could replace her as his North Star. Daredevil then told Poindexter that Kingpin was getting married that night and did not think he deserved a happy ending. Poindexter reached a breaking point, as his auditory hallucinations got worse and he slowly fell to the ground, cradling his head.{{Ref|DD313}} ====[[Battle at the Presidential Hotel]]==== [[File:DDS3E13-WalkAway.png|{{AltFloat}}|Poindexter threatens [[J. Lim]] to walk away]] {{Quote|[[Julie Barnes|Julie]] and I wish you the absolute best.|Benjamin Poindexter to [[Kingpin]]|A New Napkin}} Now aware of Kingpin's manipulation, Poindexter was determined to kill him. Donning the [[Daredevil's Suit|Daredevil Suit]] and bringing along the frozen corpse of [[Julie Barnes]], Poindexter drove to the [[Presidential Hotel]] where the venue was held. [[J. Lim]] stopped Poindexter, only to be freaked out by the corpse. Poindexter talked to Barnes as if she was alive, and told Lim to walk away and continued onwards. [[File:Poindexter crashing Fisk wedding.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Poindexter crashes [[Kingpin]]'s wedding]] Killing multiple guards throughout the building, Poindexter managed to interrupt Kingpin's and [[Vanessa Marianna]]'s dance. He mockingly made a toast to Kingpin, giving his best wishes, before throwing the microphone at Marianna to kill her. However, the attack was blocked by [[Daredevil]], which allowed Kingpin and Marianna to escape. Poindexter furiously stared at Daredevil for preventing his revenge. Kingpin's security opened fire at Poindexter, so he took cover, where he threw a plate at one agent before taking another one hostage. [[File:DDS3E13-DexAimingAtTammy.png|{{AltFloat}}|Poindexter attacks [[Tammy Hattley]]]] The hostage was freed when Daredevil attacked Poindexter from behind, wrapping a tablecloth around his head and hitting him. Daredevil went to pursue Kingpin, leaving Poindexter, who used the cutlery to kill [[Arinori]] and [[Mockta]]. Grabbing a gun, Poindexter shot [[Tammy Hattley]] in the abdomen as she crawled towards a gun. Poindexter kept his aim on his former superior, but when he pulled the trigger to kill her, he discovered that the gun had run out of bullets. As he walked away, Poindexter threw the gun behind him, hitting Hattley directly in the face without looking. [[File:DDS3E13-DexFindsVanessa.png|{{AltFloat}}|Poindexter confronts [[Vanessa Marianna]]]] Poindexter made his way to the penthouse, where he came across Marianna as she opened the door to escape. Poindexter spotted that Kingpin was too far away to interfere, so he smirked as he approached Marianna to kill her, only to be stopped again by Daredevil. Poindexter quickly knocked down Daredevil by throwing a bowl at his head, but was then attacked by Kingpin, who repeatedly slammed Poindexter against the wall. Poindexter tried to escape from Kingpin, but he was too strong, and threw Poindexter around, causing him to crash through the glass staircase. [[File:DDS3E13-DexFightingDaredevil-Penthouse.png|{{AltFloat}}|Poindexter is challenged by [[Daredevil]]]] Poindexter picked a knife, which he threw at the chandelier above Daredevil and Kingpin, causing it to fall. Both were able to dodge it, so Poindexter picked up more cutlery and viciously threw them at Kingpin, until Daredevil challenged him. Poindexter threw the cutlery at Daredevil, who skillfully blocked them. Daredevil managed to close in on Poindexter, and the two fought hand-to-hand, taking them to the balcony. However, Poindexter hit Daredevil through the window, bringing them back inside, where Poindexter threw a piece of broken glass at Kingpin, stabbing him in the side. [[File:Kingpin breaks Bullseye's spine.png|{{AltFloat}}|Poindexter is paralyzed by [[Kingpin]]]] Daredevil got back up and hit Poindexter with a series of blows, knocking him onto a chair. Poindexter looked up at Marianna and menacingly smiled at her. He took the opportunity to kill her by using a paper weight and throwing it at her. Daredevil quickly leapt and deflected the attack, saving her once again from him. Poindexter was then picked up by Kingpin, turned horizontally, and crashed into the corner or the wall, directly impacting the vertebrae in his spine. Poindexter was defeated and passed out from his injuries. [[File:Poindexter paralyzed.png|{{AltFloat}}|Poindexter is arrested by the [[NYPD]]]] The fight concluded with Daredevil defeating Kingpin, and shortly after, a team of police officers led by [[Brett Mahoney]] arrived at the penthouse to arrest Kingpin and Marianna. Regaining consciousness, Poindexter realized that he had been paralyzed in the three-way fight. As his mask was removed, Poindexter announced that he could not move. Poindexter was also taken into custody by the police. Mahoney acknowledged to the other officers that Poindexter was not Daredevil, clearing the real Daredevil from all of Poindexter's crimes while impersonating him.{{Ref|DD313}} ====Spinal Surgery==== [[File:Benjamin Poindexter's surgery.png|{{AltFloat}}|Poindexter undergoes spinal surgery]] {{Quote|The patient knew the risks. We continue.|[[Kenji Oyama]]|A New Napkin}} Poindexter was hospitalized for his injuries during the confrontation with [[Daredevil]] and [[Kingpin]]. Once he got medical treatment, Poindexter came into contact with Doctor [[Kenji Oyama]], who offered him surgery that could potentially fix his paralysis. Despite knowing that there were risk involved, Poindexter accepted his offer to undergo the surgery. [[File:Poindexter during surgery.jpeg|{{AltFloat}}|Poindexter wakes up during surgery]] Once Poindexter agreed to go through the experimental surgery on his broken spine, knowing that it could permanently paralyze him, the doctors put him under as they worked on his vertebrae, which would be reinforced with [[Cogmium]]. In the middle of the surgery, as one of the doctors discussed the procedure with Doctor Oyama, Poindexter regained consciousness. The pupils of his eyes transformed into a bullseye pattern,{{Ref|DD313}} although they eventually returned to normal.{{Ref|DDBA101}} ====Hired by Vanessa Fisk==== [[File:Vanessa Fisk and Benjamin Poindexter.png|{{AltFloat}}|Poindexter is visited by [[Vanessa Fisk]]]] {{Quote|I had you released because I need a favor."
"You want me to... kill someone.|[[Vanessa Fisk]] and Benjamin Poindexter|Straight to Hell}} Following his surgery, Poindexter was sent to a mental institution, where he was heavily medicated to keep him docile. In [[2025]], Poindexter was visited by [[Vanessa Fisk]], who informed him that her [[Kingpin|husband]] was acquitted following the [[FBI]] [[Kingpin's Federal Corruption|corruption scandal]], and she felt that he should also be released. [[File:Benjamin Poindexter is released.png|{{AltFloat}}|Poindexter agrees to kill for [[Vanessa Fisk]]]] Poindexter asked whether Kingpin knew about her visit, so Fisk explained that she had not seen him in a while. Fisk clarified that she wanted Poindexter out as she needed a favor from him, which he understood meant she wanted him to kill someone. Fisk revealed that the targets were [[Benny Cafaro]] and [[Foggy Nelson]], and that the hit needed to happen soon. Fisk told Poindexter that she was giving him a chance to get his life back, and took his medication away. Poindexter agreed to Fisk's deal and signed the appeal.{{Ref|DDBA109}} ====[[Attack on Josie's Bar]]==== [[File:Benjamin Poindexter (2025).png|{{AltFloat}}|Bullseye lines up his shot at [[Foggy Nelson]]]] {{Quote|Hello, Karen.|Bullseye to [[Karen Page]]|Heaven's Half Hour}} Now free, Poindexter transformed himself into the costumed assassin known as Bullseye, and began his mission by tracking down [[Benny Cafaro]] to [[Foggy Nelson's Apartment]], where he interrogated Cafaro for [[Foggy Nelson]]'s location before killing him. Bullseye then headed to [[Josie's Bar]], where he shot Nelson in the chest from a distance. [[File:Hello, Karen.png|{{AltFloat}}|Bullseye approaches [[Karen Page]] to kill her]] Bullseye shot two more people in the head before slowly walking towards the bar. After throwing a smoke grenade inside the bar, Bullseye approached [[Karen Page]], who he menacingly greeted, and pointed his gun at her to kill her. [[Daredevil]] arrived and stopped Bullseye by tackling him inside the bar. Bullseye released another smoke grenade into the room and instantly killed another patron with a projectile. Daredevil quickly disarmed Bullseye's gun, and the two violently fought, with Bullseye throwing several blades at Daredevil. [[File:Daredevil vs. Bullseye (Battle at Josie's).png|{{AltFloat}}|Bullseye fights [[Daredevil]] inside [[Josie's Bar]]]] As the two continued to fight, [[Cherry]] entered the bar to help, so Bullseye grabbed a glass and threw it at his head, stopping him. Bullseye was thrown into the next room by Daredevil, where he attempted to kill more patrons, so Daredevil rushed to save them. Bullseye threw a blade at one, but Daredevil moved him out of the way. A patron hit Bullseye with a billiard cue, so he threw a billiard ball directly at her face. He then grabbed another ball and was able to ricochet it to hit another patron, knocking her out. [[File:Daredevil and Bullseye.png|{{AltFloat}}|Bullseye throws several blades at [[Daredevil]]]] With Daredevil distracted by getting people to safety, Bullseye made his retreat upstairs. Bullseye was exhausted from the fight, and as he kept moving, he killed a man with a dagger and punched a woman who were going to call the police. Daredevil caught up to Bullseye, so he desperately threw several blades at the vigilante, which successfully hit and pierced his armor. Bullseye was able to use Daredevil's own [[Daredevil's Billy Club|Billy Club]] against him, allowing Bullseye to make it to the roof. [[File:Daredevil vs. Dex on the rooftop.png|{{AltFloat}}|Bullseye is brutally beaten by [[Daredevil]]]] Bullseye closed the door behind him and equipped several throwing knives, preparing himself to continue the fight, despite being exhausted. Daredevil landed on the roof behind him, so Bullseye turned around and threw his knives at him, but Daredevil was able to block them. In the same motion, Daredevil knocked Bullseye to the ground and proceeded to land several punches to Bullseye's head, pulling off his mask in the process, as Bullseye was overpowered. [[File:Daredevil vs. Benjamin Poindexter.png|{{AltFloat}}|Bullseye laughs at [[Foggy Nelson]]'s death]] Bullseye managed to hit Daredevil and get up, but Daredevil once again stopped his escape, throwing Bullseye's own knife into his back. Daredevil then continued to beat Bullseye into submission. With Bullseye unable to fight back anymore, Daredevil held him over the edge of the roof, where below Page was trying to save Nelson, who was dying. When Nelson died, Daredevil became hysterical and violently shook Bullseye, demanding to know why he killed Nelson. [[File:Dex defeated by Daredevil.png|{{AltFloat}}|Bullseye survives being thrown off a roof]] Bullseye merely laughed at Daredevil, who defeatedly pressed his head against Bullseye's. To further taunt Daredevil, Bullseye patted his enemy on the shoulder while menacingly grinning at him. With no hesitation, Daredevil pushed Bullseye off the roof to kill him, and Bullseye landed face first onto the concrete. Despite the impact, Bullseye survived, but was unable to move, and was soon arrested by the [[New York City Police Department]].{{Ref|DDBA101}} ====Life Sentences==== [[File:Benjamin Poindexter (2026).png|{{AltFloat}}|Poindexter listens to [[Matt Murdock]]'s words]] {{Quote|Mr. Poindexter, if you would like to respond, I will allow that before sentencing."
"I have nothing to say, Your Honor.|[[Maribeth Hillson]] and Benjamin Poindexter|Heaven's Half Hour}} Poindexter was taken into custody and was placed on trial. [[2026|One year after]] the [[attack on Josie's Bar]], Poindexter attended his sentencing hearing, where he nonchalantly listened to [[Matt Murdock]] give his statement about punishing Poindexter for the death of [[Foggy Nelson]]. [[File:Benjamin Poindexter sentenced to life in prison.png|{{AltFloat}}|Poindexter is given multiple life sentences]] Judge [[Maribeth Hillson]] gave Poindexter the chance to respond to what had been said about him, but he replied that he had nothing to say, which Hillson claimed to not be surprised at. Hillson commented on Poindexter's lack of remorse and sentenced him serve life in prison on eleven consecutive counts without the chance of parole, which did not faze Poindexter as he was then taken away.{{Ref|DDBA101}} [[File:Welcome To GenPop.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Poindexter is escorted inside [[Rikers Island]]]] Poindexter was sent to [[Rikers Island]] and placed under protective custody, where he was not allowed any items in his cell. Months later, in [[2027]], Poindexter was escorted from his cell, so he asked where he was being taken and tried to explain his situation, but the guards did not care. Poindexter was brought to the gen pop section of the prison, which he protested, claiming he was not supposed to be there. The guards shoved him inside, where he was faced with many other prisoners.{{Ref|DDBA108}} ====Matt Murdock's Visit==== [[File:Matt Murdock and Benjamin Poindexter.jpg|{{AltFloat}}|Poindexter asks [[Matt Murdock]] for his help]] {{Quote|You know, in another life, you might be defending me. 'Cause that's what good men do, right? Defend their worst enemies. I'm bargaining for my life here, Counselor. You want to know if [[Kingpin|Fisk]] hired me? I can tell you... but I need to be outside.|Benjamin Poindexter to [[Matt Murdock]]|Isle of Joy}} Knowing that he would not survive long in general population, Poindexter expressed that he wanted to talk to [[Matt Murdock]]. To Poindexter's surprise, Murdock visited him, which he respected, although Murdock retorted that he did not want Poindexter's respect. Murdock immediately asked why [[Wilson Fisk]] wanted him dead, as he was the one responsible for moving Poindexter from protective custody, and if Fisk hired him to kill [[Foggy Nelson]]. Poindexter responded by wondering how Murdock viewed him in comparison to other criminals. Upon Murdock's interrogation about who hired him, Poindexter explained that he would be killed soon in prison and would not be able to reveal what he knew. [[File:Thank you Counselor.png|{{AltFloat}}|Poindexter thanks [[Matt Murdock]]]] Murdock showed no sympathy to Poindexter's situation and refused to help him. Poindexter remarked that Murdock would have defended him in another life, and requested to be released in exchange for revealing who hired him. In anger, Murdock repeatedly slammed Poindexter's head against the table, which loosened Poindexter's tooth. As Murdock called in the guard to take Poindexter away, Poindexter grinned and thanked Murdock before the guard took him to the infirmary.{{Ref|DDBA108}} ====Targeting the Fisks==== [[File:Poindexter biting his own tooth.png|{{AltFloat}}|Poindexter uses his own tooth to [[Escape from Rikers Island|escape]]]] {{Quote|Ben Poindexter killed a [[Franklin Battle|prison guard]] and used his badge to [[Escape from Rikers Island|escape]].|[[Buck Cashman]] to [[Wilson Fisk]]|Isle of Joy}} Poindexter was taken to the infirmary, and as the doctor stitched him up, Poindexter detached the tooth that had been loosened from [[Matt Murdock]]'s attack. [[Franklin Battle]] went to take him back, but Poindexter smiled menacingly to reveal his loose tooth between his teeth. [[File:Benjamin Poindexter escapes on a bus.png|{{AltFloat}}|Poindexter successfully [[Escape from Rikers Island|escapes]] the [[Rikers Island|prison]]]] Poindexter spat his tooth out with lethal force, directly hitting Battle in the eye. As Battle screamed in pain, Poindexter undid his restraints, grabbed a pair of scissors, pulled them apart, and threw each blade at Battle and the doctor, killing them. Poindexter put on Battle's uniform and made his way outside where a bunch of other guards were getting on a bus to depart the prison. Using Battle's badge, Poindexter was able to go undetected and board the bus that drove him away to his freedom. [[File:Bullseye Aims for Fisk.png|{{AltFloat}}|Poindexter takes aim at [[Wilson Fisk]]]] Still wearing the stolen uniform, Poindexter headed to [[Gracie Mansion]], where [[Wilson Fisk|Wilson]] and [[Vanessa Fisk]] were holding an [[Black & White Ball|event]]. Despite the [[Anti-Vigilante Task Force]] guarding the place, Poindexter got past them and used a carving fork to kill one Task Force officer and steal his rifle. Making his way to the balcony, Poindexter took position and surveyed the dancing guests through the scope.{{Ref|DDBA108}} He initially aimed at Vanessa, but then changed his mind and targeted Wilson instead, while he was dancing with [[Heather Glenn]]. Poindexter pulled the trigger, but Murdock suddenly jumped in front of him and shielded Fisk, taking the bullet instead.{{Ref|DDBA109}} ====Laying Low==== [[File:Dex looks at broken glass.png|{{AltFloat}}|Poindexter hides from the authorities]] {{Quote|[[Anti-Vigilante Task Force|They]]'re gonna go out there and hunt down not only Poindexter, but all vigilantes!|[[Wilson Fisk]]|Straight to Hell}} As [[Wilson Fisk|Wilson]] and [[Vanessa Fisk]] got evacuated, Poindexter was forced to escape from [[Gracie Mansion]], being pursued by the [[Anti-Vigilante Task Force]].{{Ref|DDBA108}} As a result of the attack, Wilson ordered the [[New York City Police Department]] to not only get Poindexter, but also crack down on all vigilantes in [[New York City]]. Poindexter managed to evade the pursuit and laid low at an apartment, where he straightened a picture on the wall.{{Ref|DDBA109}} ==Powers and Abilities== ===Powers=== {{Quote|The [[Cogmium]] steel reinforcing framework is–"
"Can't replace pulverized vertebrae, Dr. Oyama.|[[Kenji Oyama]] and Surgeon|A New Napkin}} *'''Bionic Enhancement''': [[File:Poindexter survives.png|thumb|250px|Bullseye survives falling off a building]] Being paralyzed during the [[Battle at the Presidential Hotel]], Poindexter was brought to [[Kenji Oyama]], who worked on Poindexter's spine and gave him bionic enhancements made of [[Cogmium]] in hopes he could walk again. **'''Enhanced Durability''': Poindexter's bionic enhancements gave his body increased durability, and he was able to survive being thrown off a four-story building by [[Daredevil]], landing hard on the concrete street below. ===Abilities=== {{Quote|I've known extraordinary people. But I've never seen a talent like yours.|[[Wilson Fisk]] to Benjamin Poindexter|No Good Deed}} *'''Master Marksman''': [[File:Bullseye takes aim.png|thumb|250px|Bullseye aims his sniper rifle at [[Wilson Fisk]]]] Poindexter has extraordinary, near-inhuman talent in marksmanship; he could wield firearms with superior accuracy and precision, shooting members of the [[Albanian Syndicate]], [[Jasper Evans]], and [[Ray Nadeem]] all in the head and even ricochet bullets to hit his intended targets. [[Kingpin]] stated that he had never seen anyone with a talent like his, even after he previously admitted to being highly impressed with the [[Punisher]]'s capacity for violence. He can use practically any object as a lethal projectile. Even when he was only a child, he could drill a hole into a brick wall by repeatedly throwing a baseball in the same spot. He later ricocheted a baseball off a pole into his [[Bradley|coach]]'s head, killing him. He was also able to use the deconstructed parts of his gun to kill two [[Albanian Syndicate]] members, and stationery supplies in the [[New York Bulletin Building]], like staplers, pens, pencils, a hole punch, and a pair of scissors, which successfully impaled into Daredevil's chest, even outpacing the perception of the latter's superhuman senses. Moreover, he was able to use chandelier crystals to hurl at [[Daredevil]] and [[Ray Nadeem]] with extreme velocity and deadly precision. He also threw edible forks, spoons, and knives at several agents at [[Wilson Fisk]]'s wedding. He was even able to throw a gun backwards at [[Tammy Hattley]]'s head while he was not looking and still managed to hit his target. Other items which he threw with deadly precision, including plates, Kingpin's breakfast tray, [[Daredevil]]'s [[Daredevil's Billy Club|billy club]], fire extinguishers, a computer monitor, a trash can, a snow globe, the Bible, votive candles, a statue, rosary beads, a collection plate, beer bottles, a microphone, decorative bowls, Daredevil himself, broken glass shards, billiard balls, a beer glass, and a can of soup. Poindexter is also able to pull off curve shots and trick shots with an unerring amount of accuracy. Bullseye can hit even the smallest of targets, as shown when he killed a fly with a paper clip. He was able to kill [[Foggy Nelson]] and two cops from a distance, the latter with precise headshots. Poindexter's aim is not limited to shooting or throwing, as he was able to spit one of his own teeth with lethal force and accuracy into [[Franklin Battle]]'s eye. {{Quote|[[Kingpin|He]] found someone as fast and as skilled as I've ever seen, and I couldn't take him. He found someone to kill me.|[[Matt Murdock]] to [[Maggie Grace]]|Aftermath}} *'''Master Combatant''': [[File:Poindexter-vs-Daredevil-FirstFight.png|thumb|250px|Poindexter fights against [[Daredevil]]]] Poindexter is a highly skilled and formidable hand-to-hand combatant, having trained extensively in the [[United States Army]] and the [[FBI]]. His fighting style seems to be comprised of {{WPS|boxing}}, {{WPS|karate}}, {{WPS|jujitsu}}, {{WPS|arnis}}, {{WPS|krav maga}}, and {{WPS|muay thai}}. He was able to fight opponents such as [[Daredevil]] and [[Kingpin]], as well as quickly defeat a group of FBI agents with ease. While he is skilled, Poindexter was unable to beat Murdock in hand-to-hand combat during their first duel, largely due to the latter's greater training. Consequently, during their subsequent fights, Poindexter relied on his marksmanship and ranged attacks to even the odds. During their second fight, Poindexter fared better, though he won due to Murdock being distracted by civilians. In their third fight involving Kingpin, he was able to briefly overpower Daredevil in close-quarters combat. Years later, Poindexter was able to fight on par with [[Daredevil]] during the [[Attack on Josie's Bar]] before ultimately being bested by him. *'''Knife Mastery''': Poindexter is a highly skilled knife-thrower. He accurately threw one at a photo of [[Julie Barnes]] in a fit of rage, hitting her face, threw knives at two FBI agents during Kingpin's wedding, and threw some during his fight against Daredevil and Kingpin. He used various throwing knives against Daredevil during his fight with him at Josie's bar. He is also skilled at using knives or an improvised variant in close-quarters combat, such as attempting to stab Daredevil with a sharp piece of wood at the end of their church fight or when he used his combat knives against him during the attack on Josie's bar. *'''Staff Mastery''': Poindexter can throw [[Daredevil]]'s [[Daredevil's Billy Club|billy club]] with lethal accuracy. He killed [[Everett Starr]] by throwing it at his head, and impaled [[Paul Lantom]] from a distance, nearly doing the same to [[Foggy Nelson]] if not for Daredevil's interference. He can even use them effectively in close-quarters combat, such as his first fight against Daredevil. At Kingpin's wedding, he threw a microphone with the same form as a billy club. *'''Expert Acrobat''': Poindexter had a moderate degree of parkour skill when he escaped from the [[FBI]] after the [[Attack on the New York Bulletin Building]]. During his attack on [[Kingpin]]'s wedding, he was able to flip back up off the ground and throw projectiles in one motion. {{Quote|A self-styled assassin with a grudge, out to murder his perceived enemies.|[[Matt Murdock]]|Heaven's Half Hour}} *'''Master Assassin''': Poindexter is an extremely skilled and dangerous assassin due to his deadly talent in marksmanship. While still a child, he could easily kill an adult such as [[Bradley]] using nothing but a baseball. Poindexter was ordered by [[Wilson Fisk]] to kill [[Jasper Evans]] and [[Ray Nadeem]], which he did successfully, as well as [[Amber (New York Bulletin)|Amber]], [[Everett Starr]], [[Paul Lantom]], [[Esther Falb]], [[Arinori]], [[Mockta]], and [[Markham]]. Poindexter has also killed many other hostiles on behalf of the [[FBI]] and the [[United States Army]] working as a sniper. As Bullseye, he became more merciless, as he would take the opportunity to kill as many innocent civilians as he could, even while in the middle of a fight with [[Daredevil]]. {{Quote|I like making things happen without anybody knowing it's me who did it.|Benjamin Poindexter to [[Eileen Mercer]]|Upstairs/Downstairs}} *'''Expert Tactician''': Poindexter is a capable planner. As an agent of the FBI, he helps coordinate their missions such as transferring prisoners, guarding secure places, searching areas, and arresting suspects. He used his knowledge of FBI protocols to bypass security and ambush [[Kingpin]] at his wedding. He is skilled at tracking and gathering intel, such as when he spied on [[Julie Barnes]]. He is shown to be skilled at deception to where he can hide his psychopathy for years, nearly convinced [[Craig (Suicidal)|Craig]] to kill his own stepfather, and kept his identity as the impostor Daredevil hidden from his co-workers. He also prefers to use the environment to his advantage when in a fight, using any objects around him to throw as deadly projectiles at his opponents. To try to appeal to Fisk, Poindexter got the [[Rabbit in a Snowstorm]] from [[Esther Falb]]. However, Fisk got angry at this, saying he intended to keep Falb's wish. Poindexter only did this to become Fisk's new [[James Wesley]]. *'''Spy''': Poindexter was able to escape [[Rikers Island]] by impersonating a guard, [[Franklin Battle]], and avoided detection at the [[Black & White Ball]] until he fired his weapon. ==Equipment== ===Weapons=== {{Quote|I'm familiar with the M11. Did a couple of years in the [[United States Army|Army]]. Got real cozy with the M11. It's reliable.|Benjamin Poindexter to [[Craig (Suicidal)|Craig]]|The Perfect Game}} *'''{{WPS|SIG Sauer P226#P228 (M11)|M11}}''': During his time with the [[United States Army]], Poindexter was equipped with an M11 semi-automatic pistol due to its reliable use. *'''{{WPS|Glock 17}}''': [[File:Benjamin Poindexter meets Wilson Fisk.png|thumb|250px|Poindexter aiming his gun at [[Wilson Fisk]]]] Poindexter carried this handgun as his standard sidearm. He resorted to use it when the convoy transporting [[Wilson Fisk]] from [[Rikers Island]] to the [[Presidential Hotel]] was [[Ambush on the FBI Convoy|ambushed]], and accurately shot the members of the [[Albanian Syndicate]], even the ones who surrendered. Once he was reinstated to the [[FBI]], but this time as an operative loyal to [[Wilson Fisk]], he carried his gun while he [[Search for Daredevil|searched]] for [[Daredevil]] in the [[Clinton Church]] right after [[Duel at Clinton Church|attacking him]]. It was also the gun he used to [[Assassination of Ray Nadeem|kill]] [[Ray Nadeem]] in his own house, and when he was lured by Daredevil to a warehouse where he found [[Julie Barnes]]' corpse. *'''{{WPS|FN FNS}}''': Poindexter owned this handgun and was about to use it when he was considering killing himself for scaring [[Julie Barnes]] away and being suspended from his job, but was interrupted before doing it by a call from [[Wilson Fisk]]. Later he used the gun on [[Ray Nadeem]] and [[Daredevil]] when they broke into his apartment, ricocheting the bullets to shoot the apartment upstairs, and he lost the gun when he went to try and chase Daredevil through the fire escape. [[File:DDS3E6-DexAimingAtJasperEvens.png|thumb|250px|Poindexter with [[Karen Page]]'s pistol]] *'''{{WPS|Llama firearms#Llama|Llama III-A}}''': Poindexter took [[Karen Page]]'s gun when he [[Attack on the New York Bulletin Building|attacked]] the [[New York Bulletin Building]] dressed as [[Daredevil]], quickly disarming her when she tried to stop him with it. Poindexter took the gun and shot [[Jasper Evans]] in the head, dismantling the gun afterwards. *'''{{WPS|Glock 19}}''': Poindexter obtained one of these guns from an [[FBI]] agent as he attacked [[Wilson Fisk]]'s wedding at the [[Presidential Hotel]]. He used it to shoot [[Tammy Hattley]] in the side as she tried to reach for her gun, and as the gun ran out of ammunition, he threw it to her, knocking her down. *'''Smith & Wesson Model 952:''' Bullseye used this semi automatic handgun as his sidearm of choice during his attack on Josie's bar. It comes equipped with a silencer and red dot sight attachment. *'''{{WPS|Colt 933|Colt Model 933}}''': Poindexter and the rest of the [[FBI]] tactical agents tasked with the transport of [[Wilson Fisk]] from [[Rikers Island]] to the [[Presidential Hotel]] carried these rifles when they were [[Ambush on the FBI Convoy|ambushed]] by the [[Albanian Syndicate]], engaging in a shootout against them. *'''Baseball''': As a child, Poindexter had used his masterful pitching skills to murder [[Bradley]] with a baseball. Poindexter later used a baseball during his [[Attack on the New York Bulletin Building|first fight]] with [[Daredevil]]. *'''Knives''': Poindexter carried a plethora of throwing knives on his new tactical suit and repeatedly threw them at [[Daredevil]] during their fight at [[Josie's Bar]]. He also carries two combat knives sheathed on his right thigh which he used in close quarters combat against Daredevil before being disarmed. *'''Smoke Grenades:''' Poindexter threw smoke grenades into [[Josie's Bar]] before springing an attack on the establishment. *'''Remington 700:''' Poindexter took this scoped bolt action rifle from a guard he killed when infiltrating the Black and White Ball in order to assassinate Wilson Fisk but was intercepted by Matt Murdock. ===Other Equipment=== *'''Bulletproof Vest''': While on his missions with the [[FBI]], Poindexter wears a bulletproof vest under his jacket or FBI SWAT uniform, able to withstand ballistic impact from many calibers. *'''[[Daredevil's Suit|Daredevil Suit]]''': [[File:Daredevil (Benjamin Poindexter).jpg|thumb|250px|Poindexter fights wearing [[Daredevil's Suit]]]] After being recognized by [[Wilson Fisk]], Poindexter was given [[Daredevil]]'s impermeable armored suit and decided to take it in order to be used in the [[New York Bulletin]], in an attempt to shut down any plan to expose Fisk. The suit was created by [[Melvin Potter]], who made Fisk's suit, Potter was persuaded by Fisk to make another suit by replicating it in case Daredevil will betray him. The second suit that Poindexter equips is a red and black suit with a mask and helmet resembling the Christian Devil, leading to him taking the name and slandering it. This suit is highly durable, allowing him to be nearly unfazed by an opponent's attacks and recover more quickly from being hit, such as taking hits in the head with a punch by the real Daredevil. *'''[[Daredevil's Billy Club|Billy Club]]''': When Poindexter was asked by Wilson Fisk to put on the armored suit for him, Poindexter also utilized Daredevil's special Billy Club. It can be used as a short staff, or two individual sticks joined by a high tensile wire for grappling. It can also be split into two individual short sticks for close-quarters combat. It is kept in a holster on the suit's left thigh. *'''Tactical Suit''': While operating as Bullseye, Poindexter wore a blue tactical suit, seen during the events of the [[Attack on Josie's Bar]]. It included a balaclava to cover his identity, and black armoured gloves adorned with a silver target symbol. The entire suit was filled with holsters and pouches containing many small knives, especially all along his belt. It also contained a holster for his pistol across the chest, a larger knife on the hip, and a few gas grenades. ==Facilities== *'''[[Lyndhurst Home for Boys]]''': Poindexter was sent to live in the [[Lyndhurst Home for Boys]] after his parents passed away. He would join the baseball team until he would be sent to a psychiatrist after killing Coach [[Bradley]]. *'''[[Riviera Psychiatric Institute]]''': After the murder of Bradley, Poindexter was sent to therapy at the [[Riviera Psychiatric Institute]] for psychiatric evaluation and treatment. Poindexter was assigned to psychiatrist doctor [[Eileen Mercer]], who would help and counsel him for his violent tendencies and give him advice on how to proceed with life before Mercer passed away. {{Quote|Suicide Prevention, this is Dex. Who am I speaking with?|Benjamin Poindexter to [[Craig (Suicidal)|Craig]]|The Perfect Game}} *'''[[Brooklyn Suicide Prevention Center]]''': [[File:DDS3E5-YoureDoingAmazing.png|thumb|250px|Poindexter and [[Julie Barnes]] while at work]] Poindexter formerly worked for the [[Brooklyn Suicide Prevention Center]], an organization that answers suicide hotline calls around [[New York City]]. While working at the suicide hotline, Poindexter met [[Julie Barnes]] which resulted in him having her full attention throughout the workplace. *'''[[Benjamin Poindexter's Apartment|Apartment]]''': Poindexter's hotel apartment was his former residence that he keeps it tightly neat and clean. However, when his date with Julie Barnes went completely wrong, Poindexter began breaking his furniture due to his sanity. Eventually, Poindexter would later clean his floor from the aftermath of his rage. *'''[[New York City FBI Office]]''': Poindexter's workplace was the headquarters of the [[FBI]] Crime Division in [[New York City]], where he spent his time as an FBI agent such as socializing with his co-workers and talking to many suspects that walk into their headquarters. *'''[[Presidential Hotel]]''': While monitoring [[Wilson Fisk]], Poindexter had watched him from the security room and take orders for his routine. Once he worked for Fisk, Poindexter had visited him from time to time such as bringing his food into his penthouse. ==Prisons== {{Quote|Welcome to your new digs."
"I was told... I'm supposed to be..."
"Welcome to gen pop, Agent Poindexter.|[[Franklin Battle]] and Benjamin Poindexter|Isle of Joy}} *'''[[Rikers Island]]''': Upon receiving eleven consecutive life sentences, Poindexter was remanded to [[Rikers Island]] in [[2026]], where he was confined to a private cell under terms of enhanced supervision. Months later in [[2027]], Poindexter was transferred to general population, but escaped a few days later by killing a [[Franklin Battle|guard]] and impersonating him. ==Relationships== {{Col-begin}}{{Col-2}} ===Family=== *Father † *Mother † ===Allies=== *[[Eileen Mercer]] † - It is a common misconception that the original name is derived from the Ultimate Universe version of the character, whose name was confirmed to be Poindexter. **Poindexter's middle name, "Leonard," was another dubious name used as the character's identity in the ''[[w:c:marvel:Bullseye: Greatest Hits Vol 1|Bullseye: Greatest Hits]]'' comic miniseries. *In the [[Condemned|sixth episode]] of the first season of [[Daredevil (TV series)|Daredevil]], a [[ESU Sniper|hidden sniper]] appears for a brief moment with an ace of spades in his bag, a nod to the character's weapon of choice in the comics. When showrunner [[Steven S. DeKnight]] was asked about the scene in an interview with IGN, DeKnight said: "''I can say absolutely nothing except people have a fine, fine astute eye. Whether or not that was Bullseye, time will tell.''"[https://www.ign.com/articles/2015/04/16/marvels-daredevil-season-1-spoiler-conversation-with-showrunner-steven-s-deknight Spoiler Conversation with Showrunner Steven DeKnight] This version of the character was apparently scrapped, as Poindexter was introduced as an FBI agent in the final season. *Poindexter's apartment number is 131, a reference to Bullseye's first appearance in [[w:c:marvel:Daredevil Vol 1 131|''Daredevil'' #131]]. *The black baseball cap with a white target worn by Poindexter in the episode ''[[The Perfect Game]]'' is a reference to Bullseye's symbol on his original costume. *Poindexter adopting [[Daredevil's Suit]] and persona is a loose reference to the ''{{WPS|Born Again (comics)|Born Again}}'' storyline, where [[Kingpin]] let a mental patient loose in [[Daredevil]]'s costume to kill [[Foggy Nelson]]. **However, he did wear the suit himself while Daredevil had amnesia, believing himself to be a hero, until Daredevil donned Bullseye's suit and defeated him. *Poindexter's jacket shown in ''[[Upstairs/Downstairs]]'', when flipped up, has a white target on the collar in reference to his comic costume. *Poindexter is 6'0 tall and weighs 172 pounds.[https://marvel.propstoreauction.com/lot-details/index/catalog/198/lot/42762?url=%2Fauctions%2Fcatalog%2Fid%2F198%3Fpage%3D15 Marvel Prop Store Auction
